meaning: adj.未打上烙印的,无物主(姓名或标记)的
examples: These are not seconds, or unbranded goods, but first-quality products.
The Act protects all unbranded horses and burros on public lands from capture.
An unbranded 12-inch reference machine called Cr-48 will be available for developers — read more about that here.
Sprint branded products have to be totally unbranded to be resold in North America.
The imposition of excise tax on these unbranded players will cut into their already razor-thin margins.
